bird.name: Clark's grebe
bird.summary: Clark's grebe (Aechmophorus clarkii) is a North American waterbird species in the grebe family. Until the 1980s, it was thought to be a pal…

locations.name: 瓦哈卡州
locations.summary: 瓦哈卡州（西班牙語：Oaxaca，發音：[waˈχaka] ）是墨西哥合眾國的一個州，位於該國的南部，臨太平洋。2005年估計人口為 3,506,821人。首府瓦哈卡市。 历史 名称 瓦哈卡的州名来自其首府瓦哈卡市的名称。这个名字来自纳瓦…

locations.name: 加利福尼亚湾
locations.summary: 加利福尼亚湾（Golfo de California），当地居民多称科尔特斯海（Mar de Cortés），是下加利福尼亚半岛与美洲大陆间的一个海湾，是墨西哥的內海，面积约16万平方公里。科罗拉多河是汇入该海湾的主要河流。 參考

locations.name: 索諾拉州
locations.summary: 索諾拉州 （西班牙語：Sonora）是墨西哥三十一個州之一，西臨加利福尼亞灣，北鄰美國新墨西哥州和亞利桑那州，西北隔科羅拉多河與下加利福尼亞半島分開。首府埃莫西約，下分72市。 蒂布龙岛面积1201平方公里，无人居住。

locations.name: 哈利斯科州
locations.summary: 哈利斯科州（西班牙語：Jalisco，發音：[xaˈlisko] ），是墨西哥西南部的一個州。面積79,085 平方公里（與鄰近的州有領土爭議）。首府瓜達拉哈拉。州名來自納瓦特爾語的“Xalixco”，意思是沙地。 墨西哥最大的淡水湖查帕…

relations.subject: Clark's grebe
relations.object_id: loc-全球生物多樣性資訊機構-1874ed69
relations.object: 全球生物多樣性資訊機構
relations.evidence: In the US and Canada breeding is done across a large portion of the west of these two countries, spanning from British Columbia to Texas, for which the grebes tend to favour larger bodies of water and congregate in large flocks. Storer and Nuechterlein in 1992 dubiously claim the birds winter in Central America, as well as in Mexico and some regions of California. Out of almost 100,000 records of this taxon logged at the Global Biodiversity Information Facility there are zero records found south of northern Oaxaca, Mexico.{{cite journal |url=https://www.gbif.org/species/2482032 |title=Aechmophorus clarkii (Lawrence, 1858) |author= |date=2017 |website=GBIF Backbone Taxonomy

relations.subject: Clark's grebe
relations.evidence: In the US and Canada breeding is done across a large portion of the west of these two countries, spanning from British Columbia to Texas, for which the grebes tend to favour larger bodies of water and congregate in large flocks. Storer and Nuechterlein in 1992 dubiously claim the birds winter in Central America, as well as in Mexico and some regions of California. Out of almost 100,000 records of this taxon logged at the Global Biodiversity Information Facility there are zero records found south of northern Oaxaca, Mexico.{{cite journal |url=https://www.gbif.org/species/2482032 |title=Aechmophorus clarkii (Lawrence, 1858) |author= |date=2017 |website=GBIF Backbone Taxonomy

relations.subject: Clark's grebe
relations.evidence: Checklist dataset |publisher=GBIF Secretariat |doi=10.15468/39omei |access-date=25 November 2018}} The range of this bird in Mexico is distributed in two streaks southward; one from the California border along the Pacific coast throughout Baja California and across the gulf along the coast to southern Sonora, the other a higher altitude, inland distribution running down from the Big Bend region behind the Texas border down the mountains of central Mexico, with the highest concentration in population in the south from Jalisco to Puebla and northern Oaxaca, where the distribution abruptly ceases
